fields.brief_block: Trial strategy is judged “from counsel's perspective at the time of trial and under the particular circumstances of the case.” Butler v. State, 313 Ga. 675, 683, 872 S.E.2d 722, 731 (2022).
fields.opinion_block: the reasonableness of strategic decisions of counsel—in this case, whether to advise his client to waive his right to a jury trial—are to be “examined from counsel’s perspective at the time of trial and under the particular circumstances of the case.”
fields.finding_analysis: Butler v. State supports the proposition: in its ineffective-assistance analysis the opinion adopts and applies the standard that the reasonableness of counsel's strategic decisions is examined from counsel's perspective at the time of trial and under the particular circumstances of the case. The opinion quotes this language (from Taylor v. State) and uses it to evaluate counsel's advice to waive a jury trial, so the brief's general statement of the standard is accurate. The quote is faithful in substance; the brief omits the introductory word “examined” that immediately precedes the quoted phrase in the opinion, which accounts for the CLOSE quote-check rating but does not affect the proposition's support.
